003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.beans.*;
023import java.lang.annotation.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
026
027/**
028 * Annotation that can be applied to classes to control bean introspection and modeling.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * The bean-modeling sibling of {@code @Marshalled}.
032 * Where {@code @Marshalled} (in <c>juneau-marshall</c>) carries the marshalling-only attributes (such as
033 * {@code as}, {@code dictionary}, {@code example}, {@code implClass}, {@code interceptor}, {@code typeName},
034 * and {@code typePropertyName}), this annotation carries the attributes that describe the bean itself —
035 * which properties to include or exclude, how to name them, what stop class to use, and so on.
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Splitting the annotations lets a class participate in the bean model (for example, to declare its
039 * property list) without dragging in any marshalling concerns, and lets the bean-modeling layer live in
040 * <c>juneau-commons</c> independent of <c>juneau-marshall</c>.
041 *
042 * <p>
043 * Can be used in the following locations:
044 * <ul>
045 *    <li>Bean classes and parent interfaces.
046 *    <li>Non-bean modelled classes.
047 * </ul>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
050 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MarshalledAnnotation">@Marshalled Annotation</a>
051 * </ul>
052 */
053@Documented
054@Target({ TYPE })
055@Retention(RUNTIME)
056@Inherited
057public @interface BeanType {
058
059   /**
060    * Optional description for the exposed API.
061    *
062    * @return The annotation value.
063    * @since 9.2.0
064    */
065   String[] description() default {};
066
067   /**
068    * Bean property excludes.
069    *
070    * <p>
071    * Specifies a list of properties that should be excluded from the bean's property set.
072    *
073    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
074    * <p class='bjava'>
075    *    <jc>// Exclude the 'city' and 'state' properties from the Address class.</jc>
076    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(excludeProperties=<js>"city,state"</js>})
077    *    <jk>public class</jk> Address {...}
078    * </p>
079    *
080    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
081    *    <li class='note'>
082    *       {@link #xp()} is a shortened synonym for this value.
083    *    <li class='note'>
084    *       <b>Java Records:</b> Excluding record components is not supported during parsing.
085    *       Because records are immutable, all components must be provided to the canonical constructor.
086    *       Excluded components will be omitted from serialization output, but the parser will be unable to
087    *       instantiate the record if the excluded component values are missing from the input.
088    * </ul>
089    *
090    * @return The annotation value.
091    */
092   String excludeProperties() default "";
093
094   /**
095    * Bean factory class.
096    *
097    * <p>
098    * Specifies a {@link BeanFactory} class to use for instantiating
099    * this class instead of relying on a no-arg constructor or static {@code getInstance()} method.
100    *
101    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
102    *    <li class='jc'>{@link BeanFactory}
103    * </ul>
104    *
105    * @return The annotation value.
106    */
107   @SuppressWarnings({
108      "rawtypes" // Raw BeanFactory type required for annotation attribute declaration
109   })
110   Class<? extends BeanFactory> factory() default BeanFactory.Void.class;
111
112   /**
113    * Find fluent setters.
114    *
115    * <p>
116    * When <jk>true</jk>, fluent setters will be detected on beans.
117    *
118    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
119    * <p class='bjava'>
120    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(findFluentSetters=<jk>true</jk>)
121    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
122    *       <jk>public int</jk> getId() {...}
123    *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean id(<jk>int</jk> <jv>id</jv>) {...}
124    *    }
125    * </p>
126    *
127    * <p>
128    * Fluent setters must have the following attributes:
129    * <ul>
130    *    <li>Public.
131    *    <li>Not static.
132    *    <li>Take in one parameter.
133    *    <li>Return the bean itself.
134    * </ul>
135    *
136    * @return The annotation value.
137    */
138   boolean findFluentSetters() default false;
139
140   /**
141    * Identifies a class to be used as the interface class for this and all subclasses.
142    *
143    * <p>
144    * When specified, only the list of properties defined on the interface class will be used during serialization.
145    * Additional properties on subclasses will be ignored.
146    *
147    * <p class='bjava'>
148    *    <jc>// Parent class</jc>
149    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(interfaceClass=A.<jk>class</jk>)
150    *    <jk>public abstract class</jk> A {
151    *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>f0</jf> = <js>"f0"</js>;
152    *    }
153    *
154    *    <jc>// Sub class</jc>
155    *    <jk>public class</jk> A1 <jk>extends</jk> A {
156    *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>f1</jf> = <js>"f1"</js>;
157    *    }
158    *
159    *    <jc>// Produces "{f0:'f0'}"</jc>
160    *    String <jv>json</jv> = Json5Serializer.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> A1());
161    * </p>
162    *
163    * <p>
164    * Note that this annotation can be used on the parent class so that it filters to all child classes,
165    * or can be set individually on the child classes.
166    *
167    * @return The annotation value.
168    */
169   Class<?> interfaceClass() default void.class;
170
171   /**
172    * Synonym for {@link #properties()}.
173    *
174    * @return The annotation value.
175    */
176   String p() default "";
177
178   /**
179    * Bean property includes.
180    *
181    * <p>
182    * The set and order of names of properties associated with a bean class.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * The order specified is the same order that the entries will be returned by the bean's property set.
186    *
187    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
188    * <p class='bjava'>
189    *    <jc>// Address class with only street/city/state properties (in that order).</jc>
190    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(properties=<js>"street,city,state"</js>)
191    *    <jk>public class</jk> Address {...}
192    * </p>
193    *
194    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
195    *    <li class='note'>
196    *       {@link #p()} is a shortened synonym for this value.
197    * </ul>
198    *
199    * @return The annotation value.
200    */
201   String properties() default "";
202
203   /**
204    * Associates a {@link PropertyNamer} with this bean to tailor the names of the bean properties.
205    *
206    * <p>
207    * Property namers are used to transform bean property names from standard form to some other form.
208    *
209    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
210    * <p class='bjava'>
211    *    <jc>// Define a class with dashed-lowercase property names.</jc>
212    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(propertyNamer=PropertyNamerDLC.<jk>class</jk>)
213    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {...}
214    * </p>
215    *
216    * @return The annotation value.
217    */
218   Class<? extends PropertyNamer> propertyNamer() default PropertyNamer.Void.class;
219
220   /**
221    * Read-only bean properties.
222    *
223    * <p>
224    * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are read-only despite having valid getters.
225    * Serializers will serialize such properties as usual, but parsers will silently ignore them.
226    *
227    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
228    * <p class='bjava'>
229    *    <jc>// Exclude the 'city' and 'state' properties from being parsed, but not serialized.</jc>
230    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(readOnlyProperties=<js>"city,state"</js>})
231    *    <jk>public class</jk> Address {...}
232    * </p>
233    *
234    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
235    *    <li class='note'>
236    *       {@link #ro()} is a shortened synonym for this value.
237    * </ul>
238    *
239    * @return The annotation value.
240    */
241   String readOnlyProperties() default "";
242
243   /**
244    * Synonym for {@link #readOnlyProperties()}.
245    *
246    * @return The annotation value.
247    */
248   String ro() default "";
249
250   /**
251    * Identifies a stop class for the annotated class.
252    *
253    * <p>
254    * Identical in purpose to the stop class specified by {@link Introspector#getBeanInfo(Class, Class)}.
255    * Any properties in the stop class or in its base classes will be ignored during analysis.
256    *
257    * <p>
258    * For example, in the following class hierarchy, instances of <c>C3</c> will include property <c>p3</c>,
259    * but not <c>p1</c> or <c>p2</c>.
260    * <p class='bjava'>
261    *    <jk>public class</jk> C1 {
262    *       <jk>public int</jk> getP1();
263    *    }
264    *
265    *    <jk>public class</jk> C2 <jk>extends</jk> C1 {
266    *       <jk>public int</jk> getP2();
267    *    }
268    *
269    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(stopClass=C2.<jk>class</jk>)
270    *    <jk>public class</jk> C3 <jk>extends</jk> C2 {
271    *       <jk>public int</jk> getP3();
272    *    }
273    * </p>
274    *
275    * @return The annotation value.
276    */
277   Class<?> stopClass() default void.class;
278
279   /**
280    * Opt out of alphabetical sorting for this specific bean's properties.
281    *
282    * <p>
283    * By default, bean properties are serialized in alphabetical order.
284    * When <jk>true</jk>, properties of this bean will use the natural JVM-dependent order instead.
285    *
286    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
287    * <p class='bjava'>
288    *    <jc>// Disable sorted properties for this specific bean.</jc>
289    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(unsorted=<jk>true</jk>)
290    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {...}
291    * </p>
292    *
293    * @return The annotation value.
294    */
295   boolean unsorted() default false;
296
297   /**
298    * Synonym for {@link #writeOnlyProperties()}.
299    *
300    * @return The annotation value.
301    */
302   String wo() default "";
303
304   /**
305    * Write-only bean properties.
306    *
307    * <p>
308    * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are write-only despite having valid setters.
309    * Parsers will parse such properties as usual, but serializers will silently ignore them.
310    *
311    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
312    * <p class='bjava'>
313    *    <jc>// Exclude the 'city' and 'state' properties from being serialized, but not parsed.</jc>
314    *    <ja>@BeanType</ja>(writeOnlyProperties=<js>"city,state"</js>})
315    *    <jk>public class</jk> Address {...}
316    * </p>
317    *
318    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
319    *    <li class='note'>
320    *       {@link #wo()} is a shortened synonym for this value.
321    * </ul>
322    *
323    * @return The annotation value.
324    */
325   String writeOnlyProperties() default "";
326
327   /**
328    * Synonym for {@link #excludeProperties()}.
329    *
330    * @return The annotation value.
331    */
332   String xp() default "";
333}
